summary:
Agent found multiple references supporting that reciprocal arm taper involves multi-directional adjustments affecting more than just one axis; it impacts three-dimensionally as seen through detailed discussions about positional shifts during surgery for complex joint replacements like reverse shoulders. This supports its classification as truly volumetric change rather than unidirectional modification. To clarify further would require specific anatomical data which isn't available here directly addressing this point conclusively yet strongly suggests such nature based indirect evidences presented above regarding surgical techniques involving precise angle manipulations necessitating comprehensive spatial understanding beyond simple linear measures. Key takeaway being mutual influence among different orthogonal directions making overall transformation inherently tridimensional despite lacking explicit numerical values quantifying exact extent per dimension due limitations inherent within current dataset scope focused mainly around qualitative observations made by surgeons themselves detailing their experiences performing procedures utilizing said technology without access deeper statistical analysis comparing pre vs post op imaging results showing actual dimensional differences measured precisely along each individual coordinate system independently confirming full scale effectivity claimed theoretically herein described contextually via expert consensus formed over time observing outcomes achieved clinically after implementing design principles underlying concept known commonly referred to 'Reciprocal Arm Taper'.
